LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MARCH-21-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO OPERA ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. OFF CALENDAR. THE LEMMON DECLARATION INDICATES THAT THE PARTIES FAILED TO MEET AND CONFER "IN PERSON, BY TELEPHONE, OR BY VIDEO CONFERENCE" IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH THE CODE. THE RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY APRIL 18, 2024. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

DBA GLOBAL GOURMET CATERING, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, AND REQUEST FOR STATEMENT OF DAMAGES; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. THE UNOPPOSED MOTION IS GRANTED. JEANINE OLSON SHALL, WITHIN 10 DAYS, FULL AND COMPLETE VERIFIED RESPONSES TO AVATAR FOODS FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, AND REQUEST FOR STATEMENT OF DAMAGES. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED TO AVATAR FOODS IN THE AMOUNT OF $500. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM TOM COHEN AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM. DISCOVERY 301, THE COURT FINDS THAT OLSON HAS ENGAGED IN A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. THE COURT SANCTIONS OLSON AS FOLLOWS PURSUANT TO CCP 2023.030(D)(2), (3): THE MATTER IS STAYED IN ITS ENTIRETY (INCLUDING THE CROSS-CLAIM) UNTIL OLSON COMPLIES WITH ALL OUTSTANDING COURT ORDERS COMPELLING DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND COMPELLING PAYMENT OF SANCTIONS. OLSON MAY REVIVE THE ACTION BY MOVING TO LIFT THE STAY AND SHOWING COMPLIANCE. BECAUSE THE PASSAGE OF TIME MAY IMPEDE THE PARTIES' ABILITY TO LITIGATE THIS CASE FAIRLY, THE COURT SETS AN OUTER BOUND ON THE STAY AND WILL ISSUE A FURTHER TERMINATING SANCTION IF OLSON HAS NOT COMPLIED WITH ITS ORDERS. THE COURT ISSUES AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE DISMISSAL TO OLSON. THE OSC WILL BE SET FOR HEARING ON JULY 10, 2025, AT 9 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 301. OLSON MUST SHOW SHE HAS FULLY COMPLIED WITH OUTSTANDING COURT ORDERS COMPELLING DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND PAYMENT OF SANCTIONS BY FIVE COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING. IF SHE FAILS TO DO SO, THE MATTER WILL BE DISMISSED IN ITS ENTIRETY, INCLUDING THE CROSS-CLAIM. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)
ORDER ON DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, STAY THE MATTER IN ITS ENTIRETY

EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR AN ORDER VACATING OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, CONTINUING TRIAL DATE AND RELATED PRE-TRIAL DEADLINES, MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION OF ANAHIT TER-MAMBREYAN (TRANSACTION ID # 76119365) FILED BY DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO OPERA ASSOCIATION

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO JULY-10-2025 HEARING RE: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: DISMISSAL; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THIS COURT PREVIOUSLY FOUND PLAINTIFF JEANINE OLSON TO HAVE MISUSED THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. THE COURT STAYED THE ACTION IN ITS ENTIRETY UNTIL OLSON CAME INTO COMPLIANCE WITH HER DISCOVERY OBLIGATIONS. OLSON HAS NOT FILED PROOF OF HER COMPLIANCE WITH OUTSTANDING COURT ORDERS COMPELLING DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND PAYMENT OF SANCTIONS. THE COURT ISSUES TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND DISMISSES OLSON'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: DISMISSAL
Off Calendar Jul-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: DISMISSAL.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

This court previously found plaintiff Jeanine Olson to have misused the discovery process. The court stayed the action in its entirety until Olson came into compliance with her discovery obligations. Olson has not filed proof of her compliance with outstanding court orders compelling discovery responses and payment of sanctions. The court issues terminating sanctions and dismisses Olson's First Amended Complaint without leave to amend.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions; Declaration Of Avahit Ter-Mambreyan
Off Calendar Mar-07-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO OPERA ASSOCIATION'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions.

Defendant San Francisco Opera Association seeks terminating sanctions against Plaintiff Jeanine Olson. The Opera's papers establish that the Opera obtained an order compelling Olson to respond to discovery on January 14, 2025. Olson has not complied or responded. Olson has not opposed this motion. The Opera's motion to compel heard in January 2025 was preceded by a similar motion by cross-defendant Avatar Foods International to compel Olson to respond to discovery, which was granted on November 9, 2024.

The court finds that Olson has engaged in a misuse of the discovery process. The court sanctions Olson as follows pursuant to CCP 2023.030(d)(2), (3): The matter is stayed in its entirety (including the cross-claim) until Olson complies with all outstanding court orders compelling discovery responses and compelling payment of sanctions. Olson may revive the action by moving to lift the stay and showing compliance. Because the passage of time may impede the parties' ability to litigate this case fairly, the court sets an outer bound on the stay and will issue a further terminating sanction if Olson has not complied with its orders. The court issues an Order to Show Cause re Dismissal to Olson. The OSC will be set for hearing on July 10, 2025, at 9 a.m. in Department 301. Olson must show she has fully complied with outstanding court orders compelling discovery responses and payment of sanctions by five court days before the hearing. If she fails to do so, the matter will be dismissed in its entirety, including the cross-claim.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
